REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, PLAINTIFF TOM VERA-SWINDLE'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AND ENTER JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 664.6 IS MOOT. SETTLEMENT PAYMENT (ALBEIT ISSUED AND REMITTED LATE) MADE. SANCTIONS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $2,405.50. THE COURT NOTES THAT DEFENDANT FAILED TO COMPLY WITH SFLR 2.7B (COURTESY COPIES OF LATE-FILED OPPOSITION NOT RECEIVED). THE COURT CONSIDERED THE FILING, HOWEVER, IN THE FUTURE FAILURE TO COMPLY WITH SFLR 2.7B WILL RESULT IN FILINGS NOT BEING CONSIDERED. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: J. MURPHY; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF TOM VERA-SWINDLE'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AND ENTER JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 664.6 is moot. Settlement payment (albeit issued and remitted late) made. Sanctions granted in the amount of $2,405.50. The Court notes that Defendant failed to comply with SFLR 2.7B (courtesy copies of late-filed opposition not received). The Court considered the filing, however, in the future failure to comply with SFLR 2.7B will result in filings not being considered. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: J. Murphy; Not Reported. (D501)

Off Calendar Nov-12-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom videoconference:
Jessica Alexandra, Esq., Tel: 415.350.9795, Email: jessica@jalexandralaw.com, for Plaintiff;
Paul DesRochers, Esq., Tel: 415.362.2580, Email: Paul.Desrochers@lewisbrisbois.com, for Defendants.

Both sides stipulate to the matter being decided by the Judge Pro Tem. The Judge Pro Tem and counsel for both sides (all appearing remotely) authorize the clerk to sign the formal stipulation on their behalf.

Matter called. Arguemnt/discussions are held.

At the conclusion of argument/discussions, PLAINTIFF TOM VERA-SWINDLE'S Motion To Compel Further Responses is continued to December 21, 2021 at 1:30 p.m., for a further hearing before Judge Pro Tem Stein. JPT Stein to send the zoom invitation. By the 17th of December the parties are to advise the Court via email if the discovery disputes have resolved. The email should also set forth any remaining discovery disputes.

Mr. DesRochers is to draft an interim discovery order setting forth Judge Pro Tem Stein's directive with regard to Form Interrogatory 17.1 request/responses. The order is to be submitted to the other side within three days and provided to Judge Pro Tem Stein for review and signature.

Judge Pro Tem: Steve Stein, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
